title: Idiomatic Rust API redesign for jmap-chat
description:
The jmap-chat library is intended for third-party callers. Four API-shape problems make it feel like a typed JSON wrapper rather than a Rust-native client library. This epic tracks the full redesign.

Problems:
1. Option<Option<T>> for nullable fields — confusing, explains nothing
2. client_id in every create struct — leaks JMAP wire-protocol detail to callers  
3. Required id mixed into update structs — prevents Default::default() ergonomics
4. Method names carry _set_ prefix — chat_set_update is verbose vs chat_update

Each problem is a child epic with its own issues. All changes are breaking; do them before any public release.
